req.title: Open savings account (negative variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates a verified customer can open a savings account with a minimum $100 initial deposit and automatic tier assignment. Test design covers: (1) EP positive — valid deposit well above minimum triggers 201 + tier; (2) NEG — unauthenticated request returns 401; (3) BVA lower boundary — deposit of exactly $100 is accepted; (4) BVA below boundary — deposit of $99.99 triggers 422; (5) DT — combination of unverified customer + valid deposit returns 403, exercising the auth decision table. Each test uses a pre-seeded verified-customer fixture and is idempotent via unique customer IDs per run.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit(verified_customer_client):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 500.00}
    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("interest_tier") is not None
    assert body["balance"] == 500.00

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_returns_422
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: response body error code indicates insufficient initial deposit
trace: R-FIN-052: account must NOT be created
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_returns_422(verified_customer_client):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 50.00}
    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" not in body
    error = body.get("detail") or body.get("error") or ""
    assert error != ""

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_exactly_minimum_boundary
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-052: minimum balance of $100 is inclusive lower bound
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_exactly_minimum_boundary(verified_customer_client):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 100.00}
    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body["balance"] == 100.00
    assert body.get("interest_tier") is not None

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_one_cent_below_minimum
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: error message references minimum balance requirement

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /accounts/savings with body {initial_deposit: 500.00} and no Authorization header
expected: response body indicates missing or invalid credentials
trace: R-FIN-052: precondition customer.verified=true implies auth is required

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_tier_assignment_state_transition
actions: Capture HTTP response status and interest_tier value
expected: interest_tier value is not the base tier (e.g. tier != 'standard')
GET returns account in ACTIVE state with same tier
trace: R-FIN-052: tiered interest rates applied automatically
R-FIN-052: account created and state transitions to ACTIVE
STC: PENDING -> ACTIVE state confirmed via GET
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_tier_assignment_state_transition(verified_customer_client):
    post_resp = verified_customer_client.post(
        "/accounts/savings", json={"initial_deposit": 10000.00}
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    body = post_resp.json()
    account_id = body["account_id"]
    tier = body.get("interest_tier")
    assert tier is not None
    assert tier != "standard"
    get_resp = verified_customer_client.get(f"/accounts/savings/{account_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    get_body = get_resp.json()
    assert get_body["status"] == "ACTIVE"
    assert get_body["interest_tier"] == tier
